text: FTC alleged Rite Aid deployed facial recognition in stores and generated false-positive matches that disproportionately affected certain consumers, including people of color. Employees allegedly acted on false alerts by surveilling, searching, removing, or reporting customers.
canonical_source_conflicted: Accurate identity evidence and consumer-protection obligations
mitigation_gap: Insufficient testing, monitoring, bias controls, employee training, and consumer notice before acting on face-recognition alerts.
reliance_or_harm: Consumers were allegedly wrongly accused, embarrassed, or subjected to law-enforcement contact; FTC order banned Rite Aid from using facial recognition for five years.
